steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "In a garden, ______ the spread of unwelcome weeds such as nutgrass can be difficult because weeds usually spread easily and quickly become unmanageable." The blank must describe an action that a gardener wants to do to a weed's spread, where that action is made difficult by how quickly weeds grow.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'retrieving': 'Retrieving the spread' means recovering or fetching it — you retrieve a lost object, not a plant infestation. Nonsensical in context. ✗
  • Choice B — 'saving': 'Saving the spread' most naturally means preserving it — the opposite of what a gardener trying to control weeds would want. ✗
  • Choice C — 'halting': 'Halting the spread' means stopping it. Because weeds 'spread easily and quickly become unmanageable,' stopping them is exactly what's difficult. Fits the logic perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'pretending': 'Pretending the spread' is grammatically broken — you pretend that something is the case, not a noun like 'spread.' ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
